steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage describes Dillard's ongoing struggle to reconcile nature's contradictions. The blank sentence then delivers a resolution: nature's beauty and harshness are "inextricably linked." The transition must signal that this is the final conclusion of that struggle — the answer she ultimately arrives at.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Ultimately,': Signals a final conclusion after a process of grappling. Fits perfectly: after all that struggle, this is what she arrives at. ✓
  • Choice B — 'To that end,': Means "as a means to achieve that goal." The blank sentence isn't a method or action taken toward a goal — it's an insight reached. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Hence,': Signals a logical consequence or result. The linked nature of beauty and harshness isn't a consequence of Dillard's struggle; it's a realization she reaches. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Moreover,': Adds information without showing resolution. The passage needs a closing conclusion, not an addition. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
